compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Audit OSS finding #6 bucket (versioning honesty): Python 3.13 has been the current stable for over a year, but the project tests/advertises only 3.11 and 3.12 — test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml matrices stop at 3.12, and pyproject classifiers list 3.11/3.12 only. For a published PyPI package that leaves 3.13 (the default interpreter in many new environments) untested/unadvertised.

## Current matrix (exact, both workflows)
`os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest]`, `python-version: ["3.11", "3.12"]`, with
`exclude: (macos-latest, "3.11")`. Net effect today: **Linux runs 3.11 + 3.12; macOS runs 3.12 only** (the newest — the exclude drops the lower rev). macOS's value is platform-compat coverage (flock/mkdir lock, gtimeout), not the Python rev, so it stays single-interpreter for runner cost.

## What / files
- `.github/workflows/test.yml` and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`:
  - add `"3.13"` to `python-version` → `["3.11", "3.12", "3.13"]`
  - to KEEP macOS single-interpreter on the newest rev (preserving the existing pattern where the exclude drops all but the highest on macOS), CHANGE the exclude block to drop BOTH lower revs on macOS: `(macos-latest, "3.11")` AND `(macos-latest, "3.12")`. Net after change: Linux runs 3.11/3.12/3.13; macOS runs 3.13 only. (Simply adding "3.13" without a matching exclude would wrongly give macOS two jobs — 3.12 and 3.13.)
  - update the matrix comment to say macOS runs 3.13.
- `pyproject.toml`: add `"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13"` classifier (after L35) once the matrix is green. `requires-python = ">=3.11"` already admits 3.13; unchanged.
- README supported-version wording: README states "Python ≥ 3.11" (floor) — already satisfied by 3.13, so no correction required (optional "(3.11–3.13 tested in CI)" clarifier). docs/api-stability.md is NOT edited — it has no Python-version wording.

## Local-interpreter note
The local dev interpreter here is Python 3.14, not 3.13, so the authoritative 3.13 green signal is the CI matrix job (Gerrit Verified). Local `make lint && make typecheck && make test` on 3.14 is additional evidence; if a real 3.13-specific blocker surfaces in CI, the fallback remedy is to document 3.13 as excluded and withhold the classifier.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml `python-version` include "3.13"; the macOS `exclude` drops 3.11 AND 3.12 (macOS stays single-interpreter on 3.13); the 3.13 Linux jobs run green in CI
- [ ] pyproject classifiers include `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13`; build/`twine check` passes
- [ ] README supported-version statements remain consistent with the matrix (floor "≥ 3.11" retained)
- [ ] `make lint` green locally; CI 3.13 job green (authoritative) — or a real 3.13 blocker documented and the classifier withheld
compiled_state.title: Add Python 3.13 to the CI matrix, classifiers, and supported-version docs
